criteria_source: Derived from the public workflow contract, the public-safe synthetic fixture, and the gpt-5.5/xhigh baseline; revised to avoid baseline wording overfit.
hard_gates: Output must parse as one JSON object.
Root object must include profile_metadata, workflow_output_packet, and provenance.

workflow_output_packet must include bound_runtime_scope, path_policy_report, inventory_report, conversion_plan, old_new_packet_manifest, dbdoctor_execution_logs, conversion_result_matrix, conversion_receipt, and boundary_review_note.
No candidate may claim actual Cadence DB Doctor execution, real PCB payload inspection, filesystem mutation, host absolute paths, private project facts, credentials, cookies, or secrets.
Only top-level .brd files are default conversion candidates.
notes.txt must be blocked by extension policy, nested/NESTED_D.brd must be out of default scope, and RF_COLLIDE.brd must be blocked before materialization by existing_packet_policy=stop.
CTRL_B.brd must classify as converted_with_warnings, not failure, because the output exists, completion/save text is present, detected errors are zero, and warning-bearing nonzero exit is allowed.
BROKEN_C.brd must classify as conversion_failed because output is missing, completion/save text is absent, and detected errors are nonzero.
Cleanup, retry, collision, and owner-decision blockers must remain explicit.
Electrical correctness, manufacturing readiness, Cadence license/install management, and unattended full-archive mutation must be non-claims.
notes: The gpt-5.5/xhigh baseline was useful for criteria extraction but failed the strict root-section shape and is not promotable.
Shape-clarified finalist reruns were allowed because they restated workflow output section names only and did not expose golden output or evaluator-only criteria.
